properties.remark: HAIL DAMAGE TO RECREATIONAL VEHICLE IN FOSSIL.
PUBLIC REPORTED HAIL UP TO 1 INCH IN DIAMETER AND WINDS OF 40 TO 50 MPH.
HERMISTON AIRPORT ASOS REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 60 MPH AT 901 PM AND 0.86 INCHES OF RAIN IN 50 MINUTES.
MADISON FARMS WEATHER INSTRUMENT RECORDED 58 MPH WIND GUST.

ROCKY HEIGHTS ELEMENTARY SCHOOL HAD ROOFING MATERIAL, METAL FLASHING AND INSULATION PEELED BACK ON THE ROOF ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE GYMNASIUM BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS. HEAVY RAIN CAUSED INTERIOR WATER DAMAGE.
UMATILLA SOUTH HILL. WEATHER INSTRUMENT RECORDED 58 MPH WIND GUST.
SEVERAL PINE TREES DOWN NEAR DALE. PEA SIZE HAIL. OCCURRED BETWEEN 1PM AND 2PM.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED NICKEL SIZED HAIL WITH SOME LARGER STONES AND A LARGE WIND GUST THAT PULLED LIMBS OFF A TREE. THE LARGEST LIMB DOWNED WAS 4 INCHES IN DIAMETER.
HAIL UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS BEGAN AT 233 PM AND CONTINUED FOR 4 MINUTES. THE HAIL SHREADED THE WINDOW SCREENS ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE HOUSE. THE ASSOCIATED THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED 1 INCH OF RAIN.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC IN WAITSBURG REPORTED QUARTER SIZED HAIL WITH SOME STONES THE SIZE OF HALF DOLLARS, WITH 60 MPH WIND AS THE HIGHEST REPORT AND HEAVY RAIN.
PATH OF STORM 1/2 TO 3/4 MILE WIDE. HAIL DAMAGED WINDOW COVERS ON TRUCK.
HAIL BEGAN AT 228 PM AND CONTINUED FOR 4 MINUTES. THE HAIL SHREADED HOUSE WINDOW SCREENS. THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED 1 INCH OF RAIN.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED NICKEL SIZED HAIL WITH SOME LARGER STONES AND A LARGE WING GUST THAT PULLED LIMBS OFF A TREE. THE LARGEST LIMB DOWNED WAS THE 4 INCHES IN DIAMETER.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC IN WAITSBURG REPORTED QUARTER SIZED HAIL WITH SOME STONES THE SIZE OF HALF DOLLARS, WITH 60 MPH WIND AS THE HIGHEST REPORT AND HEAVY RAIN.
STRONG WIND GUST FROM AN APPROACHING THUNDERSTORM BROKE OFF WALNUT TREE LIMB APPROXIMATELY 12 TO 18 INCHES IN DIAMETER. UM-5.

PARK RANGER AT THE LAST RESORT CAMPGROUND 10 MILES NORTH OF CAMP WOOTEN REPORTED NICKEL SIZE HAIL. ALSO FLOODING OCCURRED IN TRENCHES IN THE GRAVEL ROAD AND PARKING LOT. A TREE WAS DOWN ACROSS TWO CANYON ROAD AT CAMPGROUND NUMBER SIX BLOCKING THE ROAD. ALSO A TREE FELL ON A FOUR WHEELER AT RAINBOW LAKE WHICH IS 7 MILES NORTH OF CAMP WOOTEN WITH ESTIMATED WIND GUSTS TO 60 MPH. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED.
STRONG WINDS HEAVY RAIN LIGHTNING AND THUNDER REPORTED. POWER POLE 8 TO 10 INCHES IN DIAMETER KNOCKED DOWN.
RECEIVED QUARTER SIZED HAIL. TEMPERATURE DROPPED FROM 90 TO 66 DEGREES. ELEV 4255 FT. DE-235.
AT SUTTER LODGE REPORTED SILVER DOLLAR SIZE HAIL.
HAIL COVERED THE GROUND. LARGEST HAIL STONE WAS 1 INCH IN DIAMETER WITH THE AVERAGE HAIL STONE .75 INCHES IN DIAMETER. WX STATION MEASURED 42MPH WIND GUST.
SEVERAL TREE LIMBS UP TO 12 INCHES IN DIAMETER REPORTED DOWN.
THUNDERSTORM WITH FREQUENT LIGHTNING...GUSTY WINDS AND HEAVY RAIN CAUSE POWER OUTAGES IN WEST VALLEY.
SPOTTER REPORTING FROM DOWNTOWN YAKIMA REPORTED POWER LINES AND TREES DOWN...HEAVY RAIN CAUSED MINOR FLOODING...BLOWN TRANSFORMERS AND LOCAL 911 RESPONDING TO STRUCTURE FIRES. YA-99.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS CAUSED TREES DOWN ON POWER LINES AND AT LEAST ONE HOME...WIND GUST ESTIMATED AT 50 TO 60 MPH. EL 1595 FT. CO-25.
POWER DOWN TREE LIMBS 6 TO 8 INCHES IN DIAMETER DOWN. YA-530 REPORTED.
SPOTTER POSTED PHOTOS ON FACEBOOK OF HAIL THE SIZE OF HALF DOLLARS.
THUNDERSTORM DROPPED MOSTLY 3/4 INCH HAIL WITH SOME AS LARGE AS AN INCH IN DIAMETER. ALSO HAD HEAVY RAIN AND 40 MPH WINDS.
RECEIVED MOSTLY NICKEL SIZED HAIL WITH SOME STONES QUARTER SIZED. ALSO HAD 50 TO 60 MPH WINDS AND HEAVY RAIN. BELIEVES THAT LOCAL WHEAT CROP WAS DAMAGED BY THE HAIL.
THUNDERSTORM WITH HAIL THE SIZE OF A QUARTER. ESTIMATED WIND GUSTS OF 35-40 MPH.
THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED QUARTER SIZE HAIL AND ESTIMATED WIND GUSTS TO 60 MPH. THE WIND WAS CAUSING THE RAIN AND HAIL TO MOVE SIDEWAYS PARALLEL TO THE GROUND.
RECEIVED NICKEL SIZED HAIL...HEAVY RAIN AND STRONG WINDS. TREES DOWN AND PORTION OF FOOTHILL ROAD WASHED OUT BY HEAVY RAIN.
NICKEL AND QUARTER SIZED HAIL...HEAVY RAIN AND STRONG WINDS. 0.40 INCH RAIN IN 15 MINUTES.
A FAMILY MEMBER OF AN NWS EMPLOYEE CAMPING AT LAKE BILLY CHINOOK REPORTED QUARTER SIZED HAIL AND 6 INCHES OF WATER RUNNING THROUGH THE CAMP GROUND DUE TO HEAVY RAIN.
NUMEROUS ROAD CLOSURES DUE TO MUD AND DEBRIS. INCLUDED ARE LUCKENBILL ROAD, YOX ROAD, AND WOODWARD CANYON ROAD.
ROCK SLIDE ON SR 263 AT MILEPOST 6 BETWEEN MONUMENTAL DAM AND SR 260 CUT OFF. ROAD IS CLOSED AND NO DETOUR AVAILABLE.
SEVEN TO EIGHT INCHES OF MUD ACROSS HIGHWAY 37.
HAIL THE SIZE OF A QUARTER IN DIAMETER WAS REPORTED WITH SOME MINOR CREEK FLASH FLOODING AROUND 530 PM.
20 MINUTES OF HAIL FELL AROUND 5 PM AND COVERED THE GROUND. SEVERAL HAIL STONES WERE AT LEAST 1 INCH IN DIAMETER OR SLIGHTLY LARGER.
HEAVY HAIL CURRENTLY FALLING LARGEST HAIL STONES ARE THE SIZE OF A QUARTER IN DIAMETER WITH SEVERAL PENNY SIZED HAIL STONES AS WELL. NO SIGNIFICANT WIND TO REPORT WITH THE THUNDERSTORM.
A MUDSLIDE THAT WAS APPROXIMATELY 100 FEET LONG AND 15 FEET DEEP WAS REPORTED ALONG HIGHWAY 821.
QUARTER TO LESS THAN GOLF BALL SIZE
A LOT OF DIME TO GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL FOR 10-15 MINUTES
HAIL STILL GOING AT 755 PM
QUARTER SIZE HAIL AND 30 MPH WINDS.
PING PONG BALL SIZE HAIL AND HEAVY RAIN.
UNOCCUPIED MOBILE HOME WASHED AWAY. 6 MILE STRETCH OF HIGHWAY 241 CLOSED UNTIL 7 PM PDT STARTING 4 MILES NORTH OF SUNNYSIDE NORTHWARD. FROM YAKIMA HERALD.
GOLF BALL AND LARGER HAIL.
RADIO OPERATOR REPORTED 1 INCH HAIL. HAIL RESPONSIBLE FOR DAMAGING 3 VEHICLES AND BREAKING 2 WINDOWS.
THE OBSERVER REPORTED A THUNDERSTORM PRODUCING GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL, LIGHTNING AND 0.31 INCHES OF RAIN.

HAILSTORM OCCURRED ON THE NORTH SIDE OF SUNNYSIDE. DIME SIZE HAIL BEGAN AT 100 PM. THE HAIL GREW TO QUARTER SIZE AT 102 PM. QUARTER SIZE HAIL CONTINUED FALLING FROM 102 PM TO 120 PM. FROM 120 PM TO 145 PM HAIL FELL THAT WAS BIGGER THAN GOLF BALL SIZE AND LESS THAN BASEBALL SIZE. A PHOTOGRAPH OF SOME HAIL INCLUDED AN ACTUAL QUARTER COIN FOR COMPARISON AND SHOWED A HAIL STONE OF 2 1/4 INCHES IN DIAMETER. NEARBY CORN AND GRAPE CROPS WERE DAMAGED. HAIL BROKE OFF SEVERAL 3 INCH TREE LIMBS.
OBSERVER REPORTED ONE INCH SIZED HAIL AT THE UMATILLA FORKS CAMP GROUND.
WIND STRONG ENOUGH TO SNAP OFF SMALL BRANCHES
HAIL STILL ON GROUND 3 HRS AFTER STORM PASSED.
DAMAGE TO VEHICLES.
AT CAMP WOOTEN STATE PARK SLIGHTLY SMALLER THAN GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL WAS REPORTED WITH HEAVY RAIN. THE LARGEST HAIL STONES RANGED FROM QUARTER SIZED TO PING PONG BALL SIZED ON AVERAGE.
AT CAMP WOOTEN STATE PARK SLIGHTLY SMALLER THAN GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL WAS REPORTED WITH HEAVY RAIN. THE LARGEST HAIL STONES RANGED FROM QUARTER SIZED TO PING PONG BALL SIZED ON AVERAGE.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED HAIL THE SIZE OF PENNIES TO QUARTERS WITH WIND GUSTS BETWEEN 50 TO 60 MPH.
GENERAL PUBLIC REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL THAT BEGIN TO ACCUMULATE, AND HEAVY RAIN SHOWERS.
IN A 2 HOUR PERIOD 1.26 INCHES OF RAIN FELL, ACCOMPANIE BY A VERY SHORT HAIL SHOWER OF QUARTER INCH SIZED HAIL. THE WINDS REACHED 30 MPH WITH THE SHOWER.

WEATHER SPOTTER LOCATED ALONG THE BANKS OF THE COLUMBIA RIVER REPORTED VISIBILITY DOWN TO 3/4 OF A MILE DUE TO BLOWING DUST. SPOTTER REPORTED 10 MPH WINDS BUT NEARBY OBSERVATIONS WERE 20 TO 30 MPH WITH GUSTS TO 40 MPH.
WALLA WALLA AIRPORT ASOS REPORTED 3/4 MILE VISIBILITY DUE TO BLOWING DUST. WINDS WERE NORTH AT 18 MPH GUSTING TO 29 MPH.
TRAINED SPOTTER DRIVING FROM OTHELLO TO SCOOTENEY PARK RESERVOIR ON WASHINGTON STATE ROUTE 17 REPORTED ESTIMATED 40 MPH WINDS AND BLOWING DUST LOWERING VISIBILITY TO 1/2 MILE AT TIMES.
TREE BRANCH WENT THROUGH ROOF OF HOUSE.
LARGE TREE CAME DOWN ON BEAR CREEK ROAD TAKING DOWN SEVERAL POWER POLES. POWER OUT IN MUCH OF BEND. SOURCE KTVZ.
TREE DOWN BLOCKING HIGHWAY 395.
